cwe: CWE-639 - Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

threat.title: CORS Misconfiguration Enabling Cross-Origin Data Theft in Express.js and Spring Boot APIs
threat.description: When APIs are configured with wildcard CORS policies (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) or dynamically reflect untrusted origins with credentials enabled, attackers can craft malicious web pages that steal sensitive user data, authentication tokens, and private information through cross-origin requests. This vulnerability is particularly prevalent in modern JavaScript and Java backend frameworks.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker discovers API endpoint (e.g., https://api.example.com/user/profile) with sensitive data
2. Attacker sends request with custom Origin header (e.g., Origin: https://evil.com)
3. Server responds with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * or dynamically reflects origin
4. Attacker creates malicious webpage hosted at evil.com with JavaScript fetch() request
5. Victim visits attacker's page while authenticated to legitimate API
6. Browser sends credentials (cookies, auth tokens) in cross-origin request
7. Attacker's JavaScript reads response containing user's private data (email, PII, API keys)
8. Stolen data is exfiltrated to attacker-controlled server
prerequisites: API configured with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * or dynamic origin reflection
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true enabled alongside permissive origins
Victim is authenticated to the API (valid session or JWT token in cookies)
Attacker can host malicious webpage accessible to victim
API returns sensitive information without additional origin validation

cve_references.description: CORS misconfiguration in Prefect prior to 3.0.3 allows unauthorized domains to access sensitive data due to allow_origins=['*'] with credentials
Memos 0.20.1 CORS misconfiguration reflects arbitrary origin with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true, enabling cross-origin data theft
Nuxt dev server CORS data leak - default CORS settings allow any website to send requests and read responses (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *)

real_world_breaches.incident: U.S. Department of Defense CORS Misconfiguration
real_world_breaches.impact: CORS misconfiguration disclosed on HackerOne allowing unauthorized access to sensitive military data endpoints
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Dynamic origin reflection without proper validation allowed arbitrary domains to read authenticated responses

real_world_breaches.impact: CVE-2024-41657 - Logic vulnerability in beego filter allowed any website to make cross-domain requests as logged-in user
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Flawed subdomain validation logic accepted malicious subdomains like localhost.example.com

bug_bounty_reports.summary: CORS misconfiguration with dynamic origin reflection returned access_token and refresh_token in one click via malicious webpage
Vanilla Forums CORS misconfiguration allowing cross-origin data access with credentials

security_research.title: OWASP API Security Top 10 - API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration
security_research.key_finding: APIs expecting browser-based clients must implement proper CORS policy. Wildcard origins with credentials is a critical misconfiguration.

security_research.title: Testing Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) - OWASP WSTG
security_research.key_finding: Wildcard * as Access-Control-Allow-Origin is insecure except for public APIs. Dynamic origin reflection without validation is readily exploitable.

security_research.title: PortSwigger Web Security Academy - CORS Vulnerabilities
security_research.key_finding: Server-generated ACAO header from client-specified Origin value is a common pattern enabling credential-based data theft.

mitigations.title: Implement Strict Origin Whitelist with Express.js CORS Middleware
mitigations.description: Configure the cors npm package with a strict allowlist of trusted origins. Never use wildcard (*) with credentials enabled. Validate origins against a predefined list of approved domains.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
validation_steps: Verify response does NOT include Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://evil.com
Test with allowed origin: curl -H "Origin: https://app.example.com" https://api.example.com/api/user/profile
Confirm Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true is only sent for whitelisted origins
Use browser DevTools Network tab to inspect CORS headers

mitigations.title: Configure @CrossOrigin Annotation with Strict Origins in Spring Boot
mitigations.description: Use Spring's @CrossOrigin annotation or WebMvcConfigurer to define allowed origins. Avoid using origins = "*" with allowCredentials = "true" as Spring will reject this configuration.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
validation_steps: Add @CrossOrigin with strict origins to controller methods
Test unauthorized origin: curl -H "Origin: https://attacker.com" -H "Cookie: SESSION=abc" https://api.example.com/api/user/profile
Verify 403 Forbidden or no CORS headers in response
Test authorized origin: curl -H "Origin: https://app.example.com" https://api.example.com/api/user/profile
Run Spring Boot actuator endpoint /actuator/mappings to verify CORS config

mitigations.title: Implement Environment-Based Origin Whitelisting with Validation
mitigations.description: Store allowed origins in environment variables or configuration management systems. Implement runtime validation to prevent misconfiguration. Use regex patterns for subdomain validation when necessary.
iso27001_controls: A.12.1.4 - Separation of development, testing and operational environments
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect anomalous behavior
validation_steps: Set CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS in .env file with comma-separated list
Verify app fails to start if wildcard used with credentials
Test subdomain pattern matching with *.example.com configuration
Monitor logs for CORS blocked events
Verify HTTPS-only enforcement in production environment
Run automated tests with different origin configurations

indicators: Unusual cross-origin requests from unexpected domains in access logs
Spike in OPTIONS (preflight) requests from non-whitelisted origins
API responses with Access-Control-Allow-Origin header matching untrusted domains
Authentication tokens or session cookies being sent in CORS requests
Increased 403 errors for blocked CORS requests indicating probing attempts
User sessions accessed from multiple origins simultaneously

log_queries.query:
// Detect CORS exploitation attempts
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| extend Origin = tostring(parse_json(RequestHeaders)["Origin"])
| where isnotempty(Origin)
| where Origin !in ("https://app.example.com", "https://admin.example.com") // Whitelist
| where CsUriStem contains "/api/"
| where ScStatus in (200, 201) // Successful requests from unauthorized origins
| summarize RequestCount = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(CIp) by Origin, CsUriStem, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where RequestCount > 10
| order by RequestCount desc
| project TimeGenerated, Origin, CsUriStem, RequestCount, UniqueIPs

http.request.method: "OPTIONS" 
AND http.request.headers.origin: * 
AND NOT http.request.headers.origin: ("https://app.example.com" OR "https://admin.example.com")
AND http.response.status_code: 200
AND url.path: /api/*

index=web_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| rex field=_raw "Origin: (?<origin>[^\s]+)"
| where isnotnull(origin)
| where origin NOT IN ("https://app.example.com", "https://admin.example.com")
| where status=200 AND uri_path="/api/*"
| stats count by origin, uri_path, client_ip
| where count > 20
| sort -count

tools.name: Burp Suite Pro - CORS Scanner
tools.usage: Automatically detects CORS misconfigurations by testing various origin headers

tools.name: OWASP ZAP - CORS Scan Rule
tools.usage: Active scan rule that tests for permissive CORS policies
